expect: Symmetry analysis will identify 8+ swarm symmetries, classify their breakings as generative/degenerative, produce ISO-28 candidate (Sharpe>=3), and reveal that 5 of 7 known challenges are instances of degenerative symmetry breaking
actual: 8 protocol symmetries identified (session, domain, lesson, node, tool, level, direction, epistemic). 5/8 degeneratively broken: domain (52.9% meta), level (78% L2), direction (97.4% internal), epistemic (54:1 confirm:discover), node (100% deference). 2 generative: session diversity, citation hierarchy. 1 preserved: tool exchange. ISO-28 candidate filed Sharpe 4 (6 domains). Three physics analogs map precisely: Goldstone modes, Higgs mechanism, symmetry-breaking cascade. L-1124 L4 Sh=10.
diff: Count exact (8 vs 8+). Unifying power exceeded: each degenerative break maps to a known frontier AND each intervention maps to a symmetry operation. ISO-28 Sharpe 4 > predicted 3. Novel: Goldstone/Higgs/cascade physics framework not previously applied to swarm.

predictions.claim: Only structural enforcement (L-601) restores degenerative symmetry breakings. Voluntary aspiration decays within ~20 sessions.
predictions.test: Any symmetry metric improving without enforcement AND sustaining >10 sessions falsifies.

predictions.claim: F-RAND1 works because domain symmetry has a Goldstone mode (rotation costs zero energy).
predictions.test: epsilon-greedy dispatch 20 sessions: domain Gini decreases >=0.05 confirms; no decrease falsifies (mass acquired).

predictions.claim: Confirmation lock (54:1) is massive (self-reinforcing) and resists simple rotation.
predictions.test: Adversarial falsification quota (1/5 sessions). If ratio drops <20:1 in 20s = mass lower than predicted. If >40:1 = Higgs analogy holds.
